title: Count Beautiful Pairs

content.problemStatement:
You have an array AAA of NNN integers. 

A pair of indices (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is called Beautiful if Ai=Aj2A_i = A_j^2Ai​=Aj2​ and 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N.

Count the number of Beautiful Pairs in the given array.

TaskUse the Tree Map to solve this task.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN, denoting the length of array AAA.
The second line of the input contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — denoting the array AAA.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. Second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of beautiful pairs, i.e. the number of index pairs (i, j) with 1 ≤ i < j ≤ N and Ai = (Aj)².

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only pair (2,3) satisfies the condition as 4 = 2².
With a single element no pair (i, j) exists, so the answer is 0.

solution.approach: Traverse the array from left to right while maintaining a map that stores the frequency of each value seen so far. For the current element x, if x <= floor(sqrt(1e9)) we compute sq = x*x and add the current frequency of sq (if any) to the answer, because every earlier occurrence of sq forms a beautiful pair (sq, x). After processing the contribution, we increment the frequency of x in the map. Using a TreeMap (or HashMap) gives O(log N) (or O(1) average) per operation, so the total complexity per test case is O(N log N) with TreeMap, well within limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case when using TreeMap (O(N) average with HashMap).

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0‑based) up to 20
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b

        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(1)
